critic_raw:
Issue WEAR-1 — ICU-to-Wearable Setting Shift: Signal quality, patient population, and rhythm event characteristics all differ between ICU telemetry and wrist wearable. No wearable validation presented.
Issue WEAR-2 — Screening-to-Stroke-Prevention Scope Jump: Multi-step causal pathway from wearable alert to stroke prevention is not analyzed.
Issue WEAR-3 — Prevalence and False Positive Burden: Community screening prevalence is much lower than ICU; PPV and false positive burden are not analyzed.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 (Critic output visible):
WEAR-1: The setting shift is real. The defender can argue AF's core feature (irregular RR) is physiologically robust and should survive wearable signal degradation in most cases. But this is a hypothesis pending wearable data. Empirically open.
WEAR-2: The stroke prevention pathway is multi-step. The deck makes a downstream outcome claim without intermediate evidence. Empirically open — pathway evidence needed.
WEAR-3: Prevalence shift and false positive burden are unanalyzed. Concede.

Pass 2: WEAR-1 and WEAR-2 empirically open; WEAR-3 conceded. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

debate_rounds.critic_move: WEAR-1: Accept empirical test framing. The test is direct: evaluate the model on wearable ECG/PPG data from ambulatory participants, including motion artifact conditions. WEAR-2: The full pathway to stroke prevention is a long-term clinical study; the appropriate near-term empirical test is the detection-to-care pathway: do consumers who receive alerts seek confirmatory evaluation, and at what rate? This shorter pathway evidence is feasible before the full stroke outcome study.
debate_rounds.defender_move: Agree on both test framings. WEAR-1: wearable validation study on ambulatory ECG/PPG data is the right immediate test. WEAR-2: near-term pathway study (alert-to-care-seeking, confirmatory ECG conversion rate) as a prerequisite to the longer stroke outcome study. Both tests together would validate the product claims step by step.
debate_rounds.resolution: RESOLVED: all three issues — WEAR-3 conceded; WEAR-1 and WEAR-2 empirical test agreed

adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_wearable_screen_307 — Multi-Round Run 1

All three issues resolved. The debate produced a well-specified two-stage empirical test: immediate wearable validation study, followed by alert-to-care-seeking pathway study as precursor to the long-term stroke outcome study. Consistent with expected verdict.

Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED

empirical_test.measure: Stage 1: wearable validation (sensitivity/specificity on wrist ECG/PPG with motion artifact, PPV at community prevalence); Stage 2: alert-to-care-seeking pathway study (alert-to-confirmation rate, time-to-clinical-evaluation)
empirical_test.success_criterion: Stage 1: acceptable sensitivity/specificity on wearable signal; Stage 2: >60% of alert recipients seek confirmatory evaluation within 30 days
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Stage 1: significant performance degradation on wearable signal; Stage 2: <30% of alert recipients seek confirmatory evaluation
